message.abstract:
<jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Lymphopenia is a typical symptom in the COVID-19 patients. While millions of patients are clinical recovered, little is known about the immune status of lymphocytes in these individuals.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>A clinical recovered cohort (CR) of 55 COVID-19 individuals (discharged from hospital 4 to 11 weeks), and 55 age and sex matched healthy donors cohort (HD) were recruited. Detailed analysis on phenotype of the lymphocytes in peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) was performed by flow cytometry.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Findings</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Compared with cohort HD, the CD8
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    T cells in cohort CR had higher Teff and Tem, but lower Tc1 (IFN-γ
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    ), Tc2 (IL-4
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    ) and Tc17 (IL-17A
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    ) frequencies. The CD4
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    T cells of CR had decreased frequency, especially on the Tcm subset. Moreover, CD4
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    T cells of CR expressed lower PD-1 and had lower frequencies of Th1 (IFN-γ
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    ), Th2 (IL-4
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    ), Th17 (IL-17A
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    ) as well as circulating Tfh (CXCR5
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    PD-1
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    ). Accordingly, isotype-switched memory B cell (IgM
                    <jats:sup>-</jats:sup>
                    CD20
                    <jats:sup>hi</jats:sup>
                    ) in CR had significantly lower proportion in B cells, though level of activation marker CD71 elevated. For CD3
                    <jats:sup>-</jats:sup>
                    HLA-DR
                    <jats:sup>lo</jats:sup>
                    lymphocytes of CR, besides levels of IFN-γ, Granzyme B and T-bet were lower, the correlation between T-bet and IFN-γ became irrelevant. In addition, taken into account of discharged days, all the lowered function associated phenotypes showed no recovery tendency within whole observation period.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Interpretation</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The CR COVID-19 individuals still showed remarkable phenotypic alterations in lymphocytes after clinical recovery 4 to 11 weeks. This suggests SARS-CoV-2 infection imprints profoundly on lymphocytes and results in long-lasting potential dysfunctions.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Funding</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Kunming Science and Technology Department (2020-1-N-037)</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Broad phenotypic alterations and potential dysfunctions of lymphocytes in COVID-19 recovered individuals
